RULING:
DEFENDANT PROBUILD COMPANY, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant ProBuild Company, LLC's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it has no liability for injury caused by products purchased and/or used by Mr. Thompson from the Lumbermen's store in Forest Grove, Oregon between 1970 and 1974. Alternatively, at a minimum the Agreement of Purchase creates a triable issue of fact for the jury. Plaintiffs' objections to Alvin Buller's declaration are overruled. Plaintiffs' objections to Maury Cuje's declaration are overruled as to paragraphs 9, 10, 11 and 12 and sustained as to paragraph 13. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. RULING:
DEFENDANT THE SHERWIN-WILLIAMS COMPANY'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE AND REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES AND COSTS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The Sherwin-Williams Company's motion to transfer venue to San Diego County is denied. Defendant's motion on the ground that no defendants reside in San Francisco is untimely; the motion on the ground that no injury occurred in San Francisco is timely. Even assuming the whole of the motion to be timely, Defendant failed to meet its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiffs commenced this action in an improper court as required by C.C.P. 397(a). Defendant's request for attorneys fees and costs is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO SPECIALLY SET A PREFERENTIAL TRIAL DATE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36(D)

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(d). Trial is September 8, 2014 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day for hearing motions for summary judgment/adjudication is September 4, 2014. Plaintiffs have stipulated to shortened notice for such motions. Motions for summary judgment/adjudication may be brought on 28 days notice, oppositions due 7 days before the hearing, replies due 3 days before the hearing. The Court will only allow 4 summary judgment/adjudication motions to be calendared per day in this case, so any party wishing to file one must make a reservation with the Court's calendar clerk prior to filing and serving its motion. For any written contention discovery already served, responses are due 20 days from this hearing or within the time dictated by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. For any written contention discovery served on or after the date of this hearing, the time to respond is shortened to 20 days. E-service is considered the equivalent of personal service. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than ly 18, 2014. Responses to the demand are due no later than July 25, 2014. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on September 4, 2014. Fact discovery cut-off date is August 22, 2014. Expert discovery cut-off date is September 5, 2014. No later than June 10, 2014 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than June 10, 2014 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on July 16, 2014 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on September 8, 2014 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by September 4, 2014 at 2:00p.m. Plaintiff to provide to Berry & Berry the Medicare authorization by 5/30 and the pathology materials by June 19. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
